translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How doth the city sit solitary [that] was full of people! She that was great among the nations is become as a widow; the princess among the provinces is become tributary!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: She weepeth sore in the night, and her tears are on her cheeks; among all her lovers she hath no comforter; all her friends have dealt treacherously with her, they are become her enemies.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Judah is gone into captivity because of affliction, and because of great servitude; she dwelleth among the nations, she findeth no rest: all her pursuers have overtaken her within the straits.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The ways of Zion do mourn, because none come to the solemn assembly: all her gates are desolate; her priests sigh, her virgins are in grief; and as for her, she is in bitterness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Her adversaries have become the head, her enemies prosper; for Jehovah hath afflicted her for the multitude of her transgressions: her children are gone into captivity before the adversary.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And from the daughter of Zion all her splendour is departed: her princes are become like harts that find no pasture; and they are gone without strength before the pursuer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: In the days of her affliction and of her wanderings, since her people fell into the hand of an adversary, and none did help her, Jerusalem remembereth all her precious things which she had in the days of old: the adversaries have seen her, they mock at her ruin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jerusalem hath grievously sinned; therefore is she removed as an impurity: all that honoured her despise her because they have seen her nakedness; and she sigheth, and turneth backward.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Her impurity was in her skirts, she remembered not her latter end; and she came down wonderfully: she hath no comforter. Jehovah, behold my affliction; for the enemy hath magnified himself.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The adversary hath spread out his hand upon all her precious things; for she hath seen the nations enter into her sanctuary, concerning whom thou didst command that they should not enter into thy congregation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All her people sigh, they seek bread; they have given their precious things for food to revive [their] soul. See, Jehovah, and consider, for I am become vile.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Is it nothing to you, all ye that pass by? Behold, and see if there be any sorrow like unto my sorrow, which is done unto me, whom Jehovah hath afflicted in the day of his fierce anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: From on high hath he sent fire into my bones, and it prevaileth against them; he hath spread a net for my feet; he hath turned me back; he hath made me desolate [and] faint all the day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The yoke of my transgressions is bound by his hand: they are wreathed, they are come up upon my neck; he hath made my strength to fail; the Lord hath delivered me into hands out of which I am not able to rise up.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Lord hath cast down all my mighty men in the midst of me; he hath called an assembly against me to crush my young men; the Lord hath trodden as a winepress the virgin daughter of Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For these things I weep; mine eye, mine eye runneth down with water: for the comforter that should revive my soul is far from me; my children are desolate, for the enemy hath prevailed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Zion spreadeth forth her hands; there is none to comfort her; Jehovah hath commanded concerning Jacob, [that] his adversaries [should be] round about him; Jerusalem is as an impurity among them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah is righteous; for I have rebelled against his commandment. Hear, I pray you, all ye peoples, and behold my sorrow. My virgins and my young men are gone into captivity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I called for my lovers, they have deceived me; my priests and mine elders have expired in the city, while they sought them food to revive their soul.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: See, Jehovah, for I am in distress, my bowels are troubled; my heart is turned within me, for I have grievously rebelled: without, the sword hath bereaved [me], within, it is as death.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They have heard that I sigh: I have no comforter: all mine enemies have heard of my calamity; they are glad that thou hast done it. Thou wilt bring the day that thou hast called, and they shall be like unto me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let all their wickedness come before thee; and do unto them, as thou hast done unto me for all my transgressions: for my sighs are many, and my heart is faint.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How hath the Lord in his anger covered the daughter of Zion with a cloud! He hath cast down from the heavens unto the earth the beauty of Israel, and remembered not his footstool in the day of his anger.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Lord hath swallowed up all the dwellings of Jacob, and hath not spared; he hath thrown down in his wrath the strongholds of the daughter of Judah: he hath brought [them] down to the ground; he hath profaned the kingdom and the princes thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath cut off in fierce anger all the horn of Israel: he hath withdrawn his right hand from before the enemy; and he burned up Jacob like a flaming fire, devouring round about.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath bent his bow like an enemy; he stood with his right hand as an adversary, and hath slain all that was pleasant to the eye: in the tent of the daughter of Zion, he hath poured out his fury like fire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Lord is become as an enemy: he hath swallowed up Israel; he hath swallowed up all her palaces; he hath destroyed his strongholds, and hath multiplied in the daughter of Judah mourning and lamentation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And he hath violently cast down his enclosure as a garden; he hath destroyed his place of assembly: Jehovah hath caused set feast and sabbath to be forgotten in Zion, and hath despised in the indignation of his anger king and priest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The Lord hath cast off his altar, he hath rejected his sanctuary; he hath given up into the hand of the enemy the walls of her palaces: they have made a noise in the house of Jehovah, as on the day of a set feast.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah hath purposed to destroy the wall of the daughter of Zion: he hath stretched out the line, he hath not withdrawn his hand from destroying; and he hath made the rampart and the wall to lament: they languish together.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Her gates are sunk into the ground; he hath destroyed and broken her bars. Her king and her princes are among the nations: the law is no [more]; her prophets also find no vision from Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The elders of the daughter of Zion sit upon the ground, they keep silence; they have cast dust upon their heads, they have girded themselves with sackcloth: the virgins of Jerusalem hang down their head to the ground.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mine eyes are consumed with tears, my bowels are troubled; my liver is poured upon the earth, because of the ruin of the daughter of my people; because infant and suckling swoon in the streets of the city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They say to their mothers, Where is corn and wine? when they swoon as the wounded in the streets of the city; when they pour out their soul into their mothers' bosom.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: What shall I take to witness for thee? what shall I liken unto thee, daughter of Jerusalem? What shall I equal to thee, that I may comfort thee, virgin daughter of Zion? For thy ruin is great as the sea: who will heal thee?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thy prophets have seen vanity and folly for thee; and they have not revealed thine iniquity, to turn away thy captivity; but have seen for thee burdens of falsehood and causes of expulsion.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All that pass by clap [their] hands at thee; they hiss and shake their head at the daughter of Jerusalem: Is this the city which they called, The perfection of beauty, The joy of the whole earth?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All thine enemies open their mouth against thee, they hiss and gnash the teeth: they say, We have swallowed [her] up; this is forsooth the day that we looked for: we have found, we have seen [it].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah hath done what he had devised; he hath fulfilled his word which he had commanded from the days of old: he hath thrown down, and hath not spared, and he hath caused the enemy to rejoice over thee; he hath set up the horn of thine adversaries.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their heart cried unto the Lord. O wall of the daughter of Zion, let tears run down like a torrent day and night: give thyself no respite; let not the apple of thine eye rest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Arise, cry out in the night, in the beginning of the watches; pour out thy heart like water before the face of the Lord: lift up thy hands toward him for the life of thy young children, who faint from hunger at the top of all the streets.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: See, Jehovah, and consider to whom thou hast done this! Shall the women eat their fruit, the infants that they nursed? Shall priest and prophet be slain in the sanctuary of the Lord?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The child and the old man lie on the ground in the streets; my virgins and my young men are fallen by the sword: thou hast slain [them] in the day of thine anger; thou hast killed, thou hast not spared.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast called up, as on the day of a set feast, my terrors on every side; and in the day of Jehovah's anger there was none that escaped or remained: those that I have nursed and brought up hath mine enemy consumed.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am the man that hath seen affliction by the rod of his wrath.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Me hath he led, and brought into darkness, and not into light.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Surely against me hath he turned again and again his hand all the day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My flesh and my skin hath he wasted away, he hath broken my bones.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath built against me, and encompassed [me] with gall and toil.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath made me to dwell in dark places as those that have been long dead.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath hedged me about that I cannot get out: he hath made my chain heavy.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even when I cry and shout, he shutteth out my prayer.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath inclosed my ways with hewn stone, he hath made my paths crooked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He is unto me [as] a bear lying in wait, a lion in secret places.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath turned aside my ways, and pulled me in pieces; he hath made me desolate.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath bent his bow, and set me as a mark for the arrow.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath caused the arrows of his quiver to enter into my reins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I am become a derision to all my people; their song all the day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath sated me with bitterness, he hath made me drunk with wormwood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He hath also broken my teeth with gravel stones, he hath covered me with ashes.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And thou hast removed my soul far off from peace: I have forgotten prosperity.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And I said, My strength is perished, and my hope in Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember thou mine affliction and my wandering, the wormwood and the gall.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: My soul hath [them] constantly in remembrance, and is humbled in me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: — This I recall to heart, therefore have I hope.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is of Jehovah's loving-kindness we are not consumed, because his compassions fail not;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: they are new every morning: great is thy faithfulness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah is my portion, saith my soul; therefore will I hope in him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah is good unto them that wait for him, to the soul [that] seeketh him.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is good that one should both wait, and that in silence, for the salvation of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: It is good for a man that he bear the yoke in his youth:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: He sitteth solitary and keepeth silence, because he hath laid it upon him;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he putteth his mouth in the dust, if so be there may be hope;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: he giveth his cheek to him that smiteth him; he is filled full with reproach.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For the Lord will not cast off for ever;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: but if he have caused grief, he will have compassion according to the multitude of his loving-kindnesses:

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: for he doth not willingly afflict or grieve the children of men.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: To crush under foot all the prisoners of the earth,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to turn aside the right of a man before the face of the Most High,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: to wrong a man in his cause, — will not the Lord see it?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Who is he that saith, and there cometh to pass, what the Lord hath not commanded?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Out of the mouth of the Most High doth not there proceed evil and good?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore doth a living man complain, a man for the punishment of his sins?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let us search and try our ways, and turn again to Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Let us lift up our heart with [our] hands untoGod in the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We have transgressed and have rebelled: thou hast not pardoned.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast covered thyself with anger, and pursued us; thou hast slain, thou hast not spared.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast covered thyself with a cloud, that prayer should not pass through.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast made us the offscouring and refuse in the midst of the peoples.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: All our enemies have opened their mouth against us.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Fear and the pit are come upon us, devastation and ruin.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mine eye runneth down with streams of water for the ruin of the daughter of my people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mine eye poureth down, and ceaseth not, without any intermission,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: till Jehovah look down and behold from the heavens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Mine eye affecteth my soul, because of all the daughters of my city.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They that are mine enemies without cause have chased me sore like a bird.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They have cut off my life in a pit, and cast a stone upon me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Waters streamed over my head; I said, I am cut off.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: I called upon thy name, Jehovah, out of the lowest pit.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast heard my voice: hide not thine ear at my sighing, at my cry.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou drewest near in the day that I called upon thee; thou saidst, Fear not.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Lord, thou hast pleaded the cause of my soul, thou hast redeemed my life.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah, thou hast seen my wrong: judge thou my cause.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast seen all their vengeance, all their imaginations against me.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou hast heard their reproach, O Jehovah, all their imaginations against me;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: the lips of those that rise up against me and their meditation against me all the day.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Behold thou their sitting down and their rising up: I am their song.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Render unto them a recompence, O Jehovah, according to the work of their hands;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: give them obduracy of heart, thy curse unto them;

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: pursue them in anger, and destroy them from under the heavens of Jehovah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: How is the gold become dim! the most pure gold changed! the stones of the sanctuary poured out at the top of all the streets!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The sons of Zion, so precious, comparable to fine gold, how are they esteemed as earthen pitchers, the work of the hands of the potter!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Even the jackals offer the breast, they give suck to their young; the daughter of my people is become cruel, like the ostriches in the wilderness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The tongue of the sucking child cleaveth to the roof of his mouth for thirst; the young children ask bread, no man breaketh it unto them.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They that fed delicately are desolate in the streets; they that were brought up in scarlet embrace dung-hills.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: And the punishment of the iniquity of the daughter of my people is greater than the reward of the sin of Sodom, which was overthrown as in a moment, and no hands were violently laid upon her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Her Nazarites were purer than snow, whiter than milk; they were more ruddy in body than rubies, their figure was as sapphire.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Their visage is darker than blackness, they are not known in the streets; their skin cleaveth to their bones, it is withered, it is become like a stick.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The slain with the sword are happier than the slain with hunger; for these pine away, stricken through for want of the fruits of the field.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The hands of pitiful women have boiled their own children: they were their meat in the ruin of the daughter of my people.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Jehovah hath accomplished his fury; he hath poured out his fierce anger, and hath kindled a fire in Zion, which hath consumed the foundations thereof.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The kings of the earth, and all the inhabitants of the world, would not have believed that the adversary and the enemy should enter into the gates of Jerusalem.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: [It is] for the sins of her prophets, [and] the iniquities of her priests, who have shed the blood of the righteous in the midst of her.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They wandered about blind in the streets; they were polluted with blood, so that men could not touch their garments.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They cried unto them, Depart! Unclean! Depart! depart, touch not! When they fled away, and wandered about, it was said among the nations, They shall no more sojourn [there].

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The face of Jehovah hath divided them; he will no more regard them. They respected not the persons of the priests, they favoured not the aged.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Our eyes still failed for our vain help; in our watching, we have watched for a nation that did not save.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They hunted our steps, that we could not go in our streets: our end is near, our days are fulfilled; for our end is come.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Our pursuers were swifter than the eagles of the heavens; they chased us hotly upon the mountains, they laid wait for us in the wilderness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The breath of our nostrils, the anointed of Jehovah, was taken in their pits; of whom we said, Under his shadow we shall live among the nations.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Rejoice and be glad, daughter of Edom, that dwellest in the land of Uz: the cup shall pass also unto thee; thou shalt be drunken, and make thyself naked.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The punishment of thine iniquity is accomplished, O daughter of Zion; he will no more carry thee away into captivity. He will visit thine iniquity, O daughter of Edom; he will discover thy sins.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Remember, O Jehovah, what is come upon us; consider, and see our reproach.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Our inheritance is turned to strangers, our houses to aliens.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We are orphans and fatherless, our mothers are as widows.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Our water have we to drink for money, our wood cometh unto us for a price.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Our pursuers are on our necks: we are weary, we have no rest.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We have given the hand to Egypt, [and] to Asshur, to be satisfied with bread.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Our fathers have sinned, [and] they are not; and we bear their iniquities.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Bondmen rule over us: there is no deliverer out of their hand.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: We have to get our bread at the risk of our lives, because of the sword of the wilderness.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Our skin gloweth like an oven, because of the burning heat of the famine.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: They have ravished the women in Zion, the maids in the cities of Judah.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Princes were hanged up by their hand; the faces of elders were not honoured.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The young men have borne the mill, and the youths have stumbled under the wood.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The elders have ceased from the gate, the young men from their music.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The joy of our heart hath ceased; our dance is turned into mourning.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: The crown is fallen from our head: woe unto us, for we have sinned!

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: For this our heart is faint; for these things our eyes have grown dim,

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Because of the mountain of Zion, which is desolate: foxes walk over it.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Thou, Jehovah, dwellest for ever; thy throne is from generation to generation.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Wherefore dost thou forget us for ever, dost thou forsake us so long time?

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Turn thou us unto thee, Jehovah, and we shall be turned; renew our days as of old.

translations.name: Darby Bible
translations.text: Or is it that thou hast utterly rejected us? Wouldest thou be exceeding wroth against us?
